pests.name: Leaf Miner
pests.impact: Adult female flies puncture leaves for feeding and egg-laying, causing white spots and water evaporation. Larvae feed inside leaves, creating thin white tunnels, which reduces photosynthesis. Wounded leaves are susceptible to secondary infections, especially Late Blight.
pests.solution: Regular field inspection. Utilize natural enemies (Hymenoptera species like Diglyphas isaea, Hemiptersenus semiabiclavus, Opius spp) by breeding them in wooden cages or on white polythene sheets. Use yellow sticky traps to control adult flies. Use a butterfly net in the morning to reduce adult population. Install a 5-foot high net around the field before cultivation. Deep plowing to destroy weeds and pupae. Remove old infected leaves and bury damaged leaves. Avoid selecting infected seedlings from the nursery. For chemical control, use recommended insecticides like Neem extract (640g/16L), Azadiractin 1% EC (16ml/16L), or Abamectin 18g/L EC (9.6ml/16L) if pest population is high.

pests.name: Potato Tuber Moth
pests.impact: In the field, larvae feed inside leaves, creating tunnels, leading to leaf destruction, bud death, and stem collapse. Adult females lay eggs on exposed tubers, damaging them. In storage, larvae tunnel into or near the surface of tubers, destroying quality. Frass from larvae is visible, indicating damage.
pests.solution: Field control: Regular field inspection. Use high-quality, uninfected seed tubers. Crop rotation to remove host plants. Plant tubers deeply (approx. 15cm) and cover well with soil. Good irrigation to block soil cracks, preventing egg-laying. Remove host weeds and maintain field sanitation. Harvest on time. If damage is severe, apply Chlorantraniliprole 20% + Thiamethoxam 20% WG (5g/16L). Storage control: Secure storage to prevent adult entry. Clean storage facilities and containers before storing. Treat cleaned storage with Pirimiphos methyl 500g/L EC (64ml/16L), Acetamiprid 200g/L SP (16g/16L), or Novaluron 100g/L EC (16ml/16L). Store uninfected, undamaged, soil-free tubers. Treat seed tubers before storage with Thiocyclam (Hydrogen Oxalate) 50% SP (40g/100kg). Monitor regularly after storage using pheromone traps.

pests.name: Cutworm
pests.impact: Larvae are active at night, cutting young plant stems at the base. In mature plants, they eat leaves and branches. Damage is more prevalent in dry weather. High larval populations in the soil during tuber development can damage tubers, reducing market value.
pests.solution: Deep plowing exposes larvae and pupae to predators like birds. Good weed control and field sanitation in and around the field. Regular field inspection to hand-pick and destroy hidden larvae when populations are low. If damage is severe, apply recommended insecticides immediately after planting: Profenophos 500g/LEC (32mL/16L), Etofenprox 100g/LEC (24mL/16L), or Chlorfluazuron 50g/LEC (16mL/16L).

pests.name: Golden Cyst Nematode
pests.impact: Low nematode populations show no symptoms. High populations cause yellowing of leaves, wilting during high temperatures, stunted plant growth, and dwarf plants. Golden cysts can be seen embedded in the roots when an infected plant is carefully uprooted.
pests.solution: Prevent spread to uninfected fields by using certified seed potatoes, avoiding contaminated equipment, and preventing entry of animals or water from infected fields. Conduct soil testing before cultivation. Implement crop rotation. For slightly infected fields (cyst count < 50), use crop rotation. If cyst count > 50, fallow the field for 2-3 years, avoiding potato or other Solanaceae crops for at least 3 seasons. For heavily infected fields, treat with chlorine powder (70kg/ha) mixed well into the soil and irrigate thoroughly. Fence off the field. Clean equipment with a chlorine solution. For infected seeds, remove all soil, soak in 1% chlorine solution for two hours, rinse well with water, dry, and then store. Monitor the crop after planting.

pests.name: Aphid
pests.impact: Acts as a virus vector, causing curled leaves, irregular growth, and distorted apices. Excretes honeydew, leading to sooty mold growth on leaves, which interferes with photosynthesis.
pests.solution: Monitor the crop from the beginning, using yellow-colored water traps. Control weeds to remove host plants. Protect natural enemies, as many are present in the environment. If the population exceeds 20 aphids per 100 leaves, apply recommended insecticides: Thiamethoxam 25% WG (5g/16L), Imidacloprid 70%WG (2g/16L), Thiocyclam (Hydrogen Oxalate) 50% SP (40g/16L), Chlorantraniliprole 20% + Thiamethoxam 20% WG (5g/16L), Buprofezin 25% SC (45ml/16L), or Azadirachtine 5%EC (16ml/16L).

pests.name: Thrips
pests.impact: Acts as a virus vector. Sucking mouthparts cause silvery streaks or scratches on leaves.
pests.solution: Control weeds to remove host plants. Protect natural enemies by avoiding unnecessary insecticide applications. Implement crop rotation. If the population is high, apply recommended insecticides: Thiamethoxam 25% WG (5g/16L), Imidacloprid 70%WG (2g/16L), Thiocyclam (Hydrogen Oxalate) 50% SP (40g/16L), Chlorantraniliprole 20% + Thiamethoxam 20% WG (5g/16L), Buprofezin 25% SC (45ml/16L), or Azadirachtine 5%EC (16ml/16L).

pests.name: Whitefly
pests.impact: Acts as a virus vector. Sucks sap from the underside of leaves. Excretes honeydew, leading to sooty mold growth on leaves, which interferes with photosynthesis.
pests.solution: Implement integrated pest management due to rapid adaptation to insecticides. Use blue or yellow sticky traps. Control weeds to remove host plants. Use sprinkler irrigation. Rotate recommended insecticides with different modes of action: Thiocyclam (Hydrogen Oxalate) 50% SP (40g/16L), Chlorantraniliprole 20% + Thiamethoxam 20% WG (5g/16L), Buprofezin 25% SC (45ml/16L), or Azadirachtine 5%EC (16ml/16L).

pests.name: White Grub
pests.impact: Larvae damage roots and stem parts of potato plants. After tubers form, larvae eat them, reducing yield and market value. Significant damage occurs during dry weather.
pests.solution: Deep plowing exposes larvae and pupae to sunlight and predatory birds. Use light traps to reduce adult beetle populations, as they are sensitive to light. Hand-pick and destroy larvae. Implement crop rotation. Maintain field sanitation. Use correctly prepared organic fertilizers in recommended quantities.

pests.name: Snails And Slugs
pests.impact: Eat young leaves and shoots, hindering crop growth. Damage is more prevalent during rainy seasons.
pests.solution: Improve field sanitation. Control weeds effectively. Remove hiding places in the field during the day. Inspect the field at night and hand-pick to destroy them. Place bait traps in various spots in the field. Apply recommended molluscicides: Metaldehyde 3% RB (10-40kg/ha), Metaldehyde 4% RB (10-40kg/ha), or Metaldehyde 5% RB (10-40kg/ha).

diseases.name: Late Blight
diseases.impact: Not explicitly described, but implied to be severe enough to warrant extensive control measures.
diseases.solution: Timely planting. Cultivate resistant varieties. Use healthy seed potatoes. Implement crop rotation with non-Solanaceae crops. Fallow the field for approximately 3 years. Use recommended fungicides such as Mancozeb, Maneb, Metalxyl, Fluazinam.

diseases.name: Early Blight
diseases.solution: Implement crop rotation. Use recommended fungicides such as Mancozeb, Maneb.

diseases.name: Potato Powdery Scab
diseases.solution: Use clean seed potatoes free from pathogens. Fallow the field for a minimum of 3 years.

diseases.name: Black Scurf
diseases.solution: Use clean seed potatoes free from pathogens. Implement crop rotation.

diseases.name: Potato Scab
diseases.solution: Use clean seed potatoes free from pathogens. Maintain soil pH between 5.0 and 5.2. Implement crop rotation. Improve soil drainage.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Not explicitly described, but mentioned as a reason for cultivation cessation in the past.
diseases.solution: Use clean seed potatoes free from pathogens. Fallow the field for a minimum of 3 years. Improve soil drainage.

diseases.name: Dry Rot
diseases.solution: Implement crop rotation. Use clean seed potatoes free from pathogens.

diseases.name: Viral Diseases
diseases.impact: Not explicitly described, but implied to be significant due to vector control.
diseases.solution: Control insect vectors of viral diseases (Whitefly, Aphid). Regularly inspect the crop and remove virus-infected plants from the field.
